name: Feature Workspace Setup
description: Create an isolated feature workspace for development with Git integration, following Microsoft's recommended branched workspace pattern for Fabric CI/CD.
prerequisites: Git repository with integration branch containing item definitions
Fabric capacity ID for the feature workspace
Git connection ID (for GitHub) or Azure DevOps credentials

steps.description: Create a new workspace for the feature branch.
steps.notes: Use a naming convention like 'feature-<name>' or '<developer>-<feature>'. The workspace needs to be assigned to a capacity.

steps.description: Connect the workspace to the feature branch.
steps.notes: Use --directory to separate item definitions from workflow files. For Azure DevOps: --provider azure-devops --org <ORG> --project <PROJECT>.

steps.description: Initialize Git connection and populate workspace from branch (Update from Git).
steps.notes: This creates workspace items from the item definitions in the branch. Use --strategy prefer-remote to take the branch as source of truth.
All items should show 'Synced' status. If any show conflicts, resolve them.

steps.description: (Optional) Activate the dev value set if the workspace has a variable library.
steps.notes: Feature workspaces typically use the 'dev' value set. Skip if no variable libraries exist.

steps.command: # Make changes in the feature workspace via Fabric portal or API
steps.description: Develop your feature in the isolated workspace.
steps.notes: All changes are tracked by Git integration.

steps.command: fabio git commit --workspace "feature-my-change" --message "feat: add new pipeline" --commit-all --wait
steps.description: Commit changes to the feature branch.
steps.notes: Use conventional commit messages. --commit-all includes all modified items.

steps.command: # Create PR to merge feature branch into integration branch (via GitHub/Azure DevOps)
steps.description: Submit pull request for code review.
steps.notes: Configure branch protection rules on the integration branch to require PR reviews.

steps.description: (Optional) Delete the feature workspace after PR is merged.
steps.notes: Keep feature branches short-lived. Delete workspace after merge to avoid drift. Alternatively, recycle it for the next feature.

tips: Feature branches should be short-lived — merge within days, not weeks, to avoid conflicts.
For selective branching (large workspaces): export only the items you need to modify.
Use fabio git checkout --workspace <WS> --branch <new-branch> to switch an existing workspace to a different branch.
For recycled workspaces: use 'fabio git checkout' to switch branches instead of creating new workspaces.
Post-sync automation: if items need rebinding after Update from Git, script it with fabio commands (e.g., activate value sets, configure default lakehouses).
For multi-workspace solutions: connect all workspaces to the same branch with different --directory values (e.g., /workspace/staging, /workspace/presentation).
Azure DevOps example: fabio git connect --workspace $WS --provider azure-devops --org myorg --project myproject --repo myrepo --branch feature/xyz --directory /workspace
